app.name: TG WS Proxy
settings.title: Settings
label.dc_hint: One rule per line, format: number:IP
label.autostart_hint: If you move the app to another folder, the autostart entry may reset.
tip.host:
Address the proxy listens on.
Usually 127.0.0.1 for localhost, 0.0.0.0 for all interfaces
tip.port: Proxy port. Telegram Desktop proxy settings must use the same port
tip.dc:
Mapping of Telegram data center (DC) number to web.telegram.org dc server IP.
Each line: «number:IP», e.g. 4:149.154.167.220. The proxy routes traffic to Telegram servers using these rules

If connection fails then fallbacks are used
tip.verbose: When enabled, more details are written to the log file — useful for troubleshooting
tip.buf_kb:
Receive/send buffer size in kilobytes.
A larger value allocates more memory per socket
tip.pool:
How many parallel WebSocket sessions per data center can be kept open.
Increasing may help under high load
tip.log_mb: Maximum log file size; the file is overwritten when the limit is reached
tip.autostart: Launch TG WS Proxy on Windows login. If you move the app to another folder, autostart will reset
tip.cfproxy: Use Cloudflare proxy for unreachable data centers
tip.cfproxy_domain:
Your own domains proxied through Cloudflare for WS connections.
Separate multiple domains with commas.
If empty — chosen automatically from supported domains
tip.cfproxy_user_domain_cb: Specify your own domains instead of automatic selection
tip.cfworker_domain:
Cloudflare Worker domains (e.g. name.account.workers.dev).
Separate multiple domains with commas.
The proxy routes connections to Telegram DCs by IP through them
validation.bad_port: Port must be a number between 1 and 65535
validation.bad_secret_len: Secret must be exactly 32 hex characters (16 bytes).
validation.bad_secret_hex: Secret must contain only hex characters (0-9, a-f).
connectivity.cf_auto_fail: ✗ None of the automatic CF domains respond.
updates.status_error: Could not reach GitHub. Check your network.
updates.status_pending: Status will appear after the background check on startup.
updates.status_available: Version {latest} is available on GitHub (you have {current}).
updates.status_ahead: You have {current} — newer than the latest GitHub release ({latest}).
updates.status_latest: Latest known version from GitHub is installed.
first_run.title: Proxy is running in the system tray
first_run.auto_link: Or copy the link, send it to yourself in TG and click it: {url}
dialog.restart_body:
Settings saved.

Restart the proxy now?
dialog.log_not_found: Log file has not been created yet.

dialog.pyperclip_missing: Install pyperclip to copy to clipboard.
update.ask_open:
New version available: {version}

Open the release page in the browser?
diagnostics.port_busy:
Failed to start proxy:
Port is already in use by another application.

Close the app using this port, or change the port in proxy settings and restart.
diagnostics.permission:
Failed to start proxy:
Access to address/port denied (firewall, antivirus, or permissions).

Change the port to a random value in 10000–50000 in settings, check firewall/antivirus, and restart.
diagnostics.bad_address:
Failed to start proxy:
Invalid or unavailable listen address.

Check the solution at the link opened in your browser.
Verify host and port in proxy settings and restart.
ipv6.warning:
IPv6 connectivity is enabled on your computer.

Telegram may try to connect over IPv6, which is not supported and may cause errors.

If the proxy does not work or logs show IPv6 connection attempts, try disabling IPv6 connection attempts in Telegram proxy settings. If that does not help, try disabling IPv6 system-wide.

This warning is shown only once.
